content:
🚀 Google-Maps-Scrapper | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automated extraction of business information from Google Maps for analysis and integration into other systems.
✨ Key Features: • Scrapes business data from Google Maps (name, address, website, phone, reviews, hours, etc.) • Extracts review counts and average ratings • Detects business service types (in-store shopping, pickup, delivery) • Extracts introductory business information • Cleanses and organizes scraped data
📖 Summary: This Python repository uses Playwright to scrape business information from Google Maps, extracting details like name, address, website, reviews, and operating hours. It cleans the data and exports it to a CSV file for further analysis.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 ssh-ai-chat | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a seamless and accessible way for users to chat with AI models directly from their terminal via SSH, without needing a graphical interface.
✨ Key Features: • Chat with AI over SSH • Supported on macOS (iTerm2, Ghostty) • Backend using Node.js and SSH2 • UI Framework using React and Ink • Database support for PostgreSQL and Redis
📖 Summary: SSH AI Chat allows users to interact with AI models directly through an SSH client. It provides a command-line interface experience for chatting with AI, supporting various terminals and offering deployment options via Docker.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 lsv | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: A fast, keyboard-centric, and highly configurable file manager for the terminal, enhancing productivity for developers and power users.
✨ Key Features: • Three-pane navigation (parent/current/preview) • Keyboard-driven UX with multi-key sequences and which-key overlay • Lua configuration for themes, keymaps, actions, and previewer • External command integration for previews • File operations (add/rename/delete, copy/move/paste)
📖 Summary: lsv is a terminal-based file browser written in Rust, offering a three-pane interface for navigating parent, current, and preview directories. It's highly configurable via Lua and designed for keyboard-driven interaction with features like multi-key sequences and a which-key overlay.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ EternalVows | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating a personalized, self-hosted wedding website to share event information.
✨ Key Features: • Customizable, self-hosted wedding website template • Config-driven content via YAML or JSON (names, date, location, story, schedule, venues, registry, FAQs) • Dynamic background slideshow with auto-refreshing images • Clean, modern single-page design • Lightweight Node.js + Express server
📖 Summary: Eternal Vows is a lightweight, self-hosted wedding website template. It allows users to easily configure and display all essential wedding details, such as names, dates, venues, stories, schedules, and registry links, through a simple YAML or JSON file. The template features a clean, modern design with a dynamic background slideshow powered by a Node.js server.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 guarddog | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To scan and identify malicious software packages in various development ecosystems, enhancing software supply chain security.
✨ Key Features: • Identifies malicious PyPI and npm packages • Supports Go modules, RubyGems, GitHub actions, and VSCode extensions • Scans local or remote packages
📖 Summary: GuardDog is a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed to identify malicious packages across various ecosystems, including PyPI, npm, Go, RubyGems, GitHub Actions, and VSCode extensions. It achieves this by analyzing package source code using Semgrep rules and examining package metadata to detect potential threats.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ guarddog | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To scan and identify malicious software packages within the software supply chain across multiple programming language ecosystems.
✨ Key Features: • Identifies malicious PyPI and npm packages • Supports Go modules, RubyGems, GitHub actions, and VSCode extensions • Scans local or remote packages
📖 Summary: GuardDog is a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed to identify malicious packages across various ecosystems including PyPI, npm, Go, RubyGems, GitHub Actions, and VSCode extensions. It employs heuristics and Semgrep rules to analyze both package source code and metadata for potential threats.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
